title: Smoky German Wagyu Stew
description: A smoky, gooey German-inspired stew featuring premium wagyu beef, confit tomato, and low-carb-friendly vegetables for a romantic dinner.
instructions: Heat a heavy skillet over high heat and sear the wagyu cubes until browned on all sides, about 2-3 minutes per side, then transfer to a pot.
In the same skillet, add olive oil and sauté minced garlic until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Add diced onion, smoked paprika, and sliced red bell pepper to the skillet, cooking until onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in tomato paste, diced confit tomato, and a splash of red wine, letting the mixture reduce slightly for 2 minutes.
Pour in the beef broth and bring the liquid to a gentle simmer, then place the seared wagyu and vegetables back into the pot.
Add the sliced zucchini and thyme sprigs, bring the stew to a boil, then reduce heat to low and let it simmer, uncovered, for 45 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper to taste, then remove the thyme sprigs before serving.
Stir the stew gently to combine flavors and adjust consistency with a splash of broth if needed.
Ladle the stew into bowls, garnish with fresh parsley if desired, and serve hot.
Enjoy this smoky, gooey German wagyu stew as a romantic low-carb dinner.
